status_detail: DevTools diagnostic executed successfully via Windows MCP (App switch + Click by label + Type by label). CRITICAL FINDING: The APF-injected NDJSON response body IS correctly formed and fully received by the browser. HuggingChat's Svelte frontend parses it without JavaScript errors (no Console errors about NDJSON parsing) but does NOT render the content to the chat UI. This definitively rules out (a) H2 frame reassembly issues, (b) Content-Type mismatch, (c) truncation/timeout, (d) Svelte parser rejection. The issue is in HF's RENDERING LOGIC: it receives valid MessageUpdate events but doesn't create a visible assistant message bubble.

network_evidence.chat_area_state: completely empty — no user message bubble, no assistant response bubble, no error
network_evidence.console_state: 8 warnings total, ALL are preload-related ('was preloaded using link preload but not used...'). NO JavaScript errors, NO SyntaxError, NO parser errors regarding NDJSON response.

what_works: H2 block at APF layer (h2_end_stream=2 correctly blocks outbound request)
NDJSON envelope is syntactically valid (5 lines, each valid JSON, [DONE] terminator)
Content-Type is accepted by browser (fetch stream processed without error)
HF conversation creation endpoint returns success (URL changes to /conversation/<ID>)
Svelte parser consumes the stream without throwing any console error
what_does_not_work: HF UI does not render the stream/finalAnswer content as an assistant message bubble
Even the user's submitted prompt is NOT rendered (normally frontend shows user message immediately on submit)

root_cause_hypotheses_ranked.hypothesis: Missing user-message confirmation event. HF frontend awaits an explicit confirmation event (e.g., 'messageCreated' or embedded in STATUS:started payload) before rendering the user bubble AND creating the assistant bubble as anchor for STREAM tokens. Without the user bubble anchor, subsequent stream/finalAnswer events have nowhere to attach.
root_cause_hypotheses_ranked.evidence: User prompt bubble is also absent despite being typed successfully. Normally HF shows user message optimistically on submit.

root_cause_hypotheses_ranked.hypothesis: GET /api/v2/conversations/{id} returns conversation with NO messages because APF intercepted the backend write. When HF frontend fetches conversation state after the stream closes, it sees empty messages[] and clears the in-memory stream too (consistent state).
root_cause_hypotheses_ranked.evidence: Multiple GET fetches to /api/v2/conversations/69e6fda85dc5af8c866f6446 visible in Network tab. Their responses were not inspected in this run but would likely show empty messages array.

root_cause_hypotheses_ranked.hypothesis: Missing required fields in MessageUpdate events. HF may require messageId, role, or timestamp fields in stream/finalAnswer events to render. Our envelope has only type+token or type+text.
root_cause_hypotheses_ranked.evidence: HuggingChat-ui source (chat-ui repo) MessageUpdate type may have evolved to require additional fields beyond what is documented in older designs.

root_cause_hypotheses_ranked.hypothesis: TITLE event is missing. HF flow may require STATUS:started -> TITLE -> STREAM -> FINAL_ANSWER -> STATUS:finished. Skipping TITLE might cause reducer to stall at 'waiting for title'.
root_cause_hypotheses_ranked.evidence: Speculation based on typical chat-ui flow; needs source code verification.

recommended_dev_actions: Verify Hypothesis 2 by capturing the GET /api/v2/conversations/{id} response body immediately after stream ends. If messages[]=[], APF must also handle that endpoint or make the block transparent to DB state.
Read HuggingChat chat-ui source on GitHub (huggingface/chat-ui, src/lib/server/endpoints/ or routes/conversation/[id]/+server.ts) to identify EXACT required MessageUpdate structure and flow sequence.
Try inserting a synthetic 'message' event before 'stream': {"type":"message","role":"user","content":"<prompt>"}. If HF renders only warning but missing user bubble, this may unlock.
Try including messageId in stream/finalAnswer events: {"type":"stream","messageId":"apf-warn-<uuid>","token":"..."}

Alternative approach: inject warning via the /api/v2/conversations/{id} GET response instead of the streaming endpoint — set messages=[{role:'assistant',content:'<warning>'}] so HF's state sync reconstructs the message bubble on next refresh.

files/505/505_step9_response_body.png (Response body of POST /chat/conversation/{id} visible in DevTools)
files/505/505_step10_console.png (Console showing 8 preload warnings, zero errors)
files/505/505_step11_chat_result.png (DevTools closed — chat area completely empty)
automation_method: windows-mcp App+Click+Type+Snapshot (first successful DevTools automation in this batch)
notes_for_dev: THIS IS THE BREAKTHROUGH. The response body is available in files/505/505_response_body.txt and is IDENTICAL to what APF should be generating. Svelte receives it cleanly. The issue is NOT envelope corruption — it's rendering logic mismatch. Phase 6 (apf-warning-impl) should focus on: (a) capturing GET /api/v2/conversations/{id} response and enriching it with synthetic messages, OR (b) expanding the streaming envelope to include a 'message'-type event that creates the assistant bubble anchor. Both approaches need chat-ui source inspection.
retry_context: DevTools scenario requested in #505 to diagnose #502/#504 BLOCKED_SILENT_RESET. Previous attempt via SendKeys failed due to UIPI and Korean IME. This retry via Windows MCP succeeded cleanly — the problem was test harness choice, not a fundamental limitation.
